directive.title: RBAC role column and middleware-free route authorization

auditor.summary: The build implements the core RBAC functionality correctly with proper role enforcement, audit logging, and integration tests. However, it has a CRITICAL directive violation by creating a new file under /api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts, which is explicitly forbidden. Additionally, there are CRITICAL buildability issues with an unlisted npm dependency ('postgres' not in package.json), and HIGH-severity issues with audit logging not capturing full request context and test isolation violating the INSERT-only constraint. The build is recoverable but requires significant fixes before it can pass.

top_issues.description: DIRECTIVE VIOLATION: The directive explicitly states 'No modifications to middleware.ts, /api/auth/**, or CI/CD workflows' and constraint 'MUST NOT modify any file matching /api/auth/**'. Creating a new file under /api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts is a modification of the /api/auth/ path. This is a hard constraint violation that invalidates the build.

BUILDABILITY: Imports '@/lib/db' which imports 'postgres' package. The package 'postgres' is NOT listed in package.json dependencies. Available packages include 'pg' but not 'postgres'. This will cause a module resolution failure at compile time.

AUDIT LOGGING FLAW: The logAuthorizationDenial function uses hardcoded 'unknown' for resourcePath and method instead of the actual request context. The directive requires 'full context (user_id, resolved_role, required_roles, resource_path, HTTP method, UTC timestamp, UUID trace_id)'. The resource_path and method are not being captured from the actual request.
